summary: High‑energy 1968 Avalon Ballroom show featuring a smoking Dark Star and flawless Eleven, captured on a crisp vintage soundboard despite a few pitch quirks.
review: The performance is hailed as one of the band’s classic late‑summer ’68 outings – Dark Star, The Eleven, New Potato > Drums, Other One and Death Don’t Have No Mercy all receive glowing praise for their intensity and ensemble interplay.  The Charley Miller soundboard source delivers clear vintage sound, though a handful of listeners note a slight pitch issue and an abrupt splice at the end of New Potato.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high 5‑star reviews praise the musical performance; recording is generally praised as clear soundboard with only minor technical complaints, yielding a strong overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally crisp vintage soundboard capture; several reviewers call it “great recording.” One reviewer mentions the track is slightly short and needs pitch correction, and another notes a possible splice at New Potato > Drums.

show_quality.setlist_flow: High‑energy, especially in the second set; seamless transitions praised except for an abrupt ending of New Potato that some find jarring.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly lauded for masterful guitar work on Dark Star and Death Don't Have No Mercy.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass highlighted as feeding off the drummers and contributing to the tight ensemble sound.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drummers described as fabulous; New Potato > Drums jam cited as a highlight, though one reviewer found the pure ‘Drums’ segment less interesting.
